observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Officer (GSO) continue reconciling their records and make the necessary
adjustments or corrections in the books and property records to ensure
accuracy and reliability in the PPE account.

1.12. Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and
maintain complete and detailed PPE ledger cards for all LGU properties to
enhance record-keeping and accountability.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the completed projects
that are still in the Construction in Progress - Infrastructure Assets,
Construction in Progress - Buildings and Other Structures and Construction
Materials Inventory accounts to their appropriate PPE accounts and record the
corresponding depreciation.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant drop the aforementioned
properties totaling ₱16,301,600.64 from the PPE account to arrive at the
correct account balance.

3.12. We also recommended that, moving forward, the Municipal Accountant
comply with COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 and COA
Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 for the proper treatment of semi-
expendable properties.

4.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the General Fund
books the transfer of Motor Vehicles from Trust Fund totaling ₱1,403,239.09.

5.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Municipal
Engineer reconcile their Road Network records.

5.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal
Accountant and the GSO with the correct and complete description and cost
segregation of road components of all road projects.

5.10. Moreover, we recommended that the OIC Municipal Assessor inspect and
assess the cost of the road lot component of all road networks within the
territorial jurisdiction of LGU-Javier and provide data to the Municipal
Accountant, GSO and Municipal Engineer.

5.11. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the Road Lot
component of the Municipal Road projects based on the report of the OIC-
Municipal Assessor, maintain the LRNLC with complete description of each road
component, and Disclose in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years the breakdown of
the Road Network Account.

5.12. Finally, we recommended that the GSO prepare and maintain complete and
detailed LRNPC for all roads and its components.

6.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer and record the
unexpended DRRM Fund amounting to ₱2,378,788.77 as Trust Liabilities -
DRRMF under the Special Trust Fund.

7.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer,
identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimum utilization of
the 20% Development Fund.

8.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned
submit written explanation/justification on the improper
reversion/realignment without supplemental budget and appropriation
ordinance.

8.14. We also recommended that the Sanggunian and the Presiding Officer submit
written explanation/justification on the approval of reversion/realignment
through SB resolutions instead of appropriation ordinance and despite lacking
supplemental budget.

9.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, GAD Executive Committee,
GAD Technical Working Group and GAD Focal Person strictly adhere to the
provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01, as amended, to ensure that all programs, projects, and activities are
implemented, thereby achieving the intended objectives of the attributed
funds.

10.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all insurable LGU
properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the
Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No.
656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002.

11.6 We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
(HRMO):
a. Reconcile records with GSIS and promptly update employees on any
necessary changes to their employment status. These updates should be
communicated to the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) using the
prescribed GSIS format to ensure timely and accurate record updates;
and
b. Ensure that all premium contributions and loan amortizations are
deducted from employees' salaries in the payroll for the month they are
due.

11.7 We also recommended that the AAO:
a. Promptly update employees' employment status and salary
adjustments in the GSIS Web MPS upon receiving the necessary
information from the HRMO; and
b. Monitor the approval of employees' loan applications and immediately
inform the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely
deductions from payroll.

11.8 Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. provide the Audit Team with an explanation of the discrepancies
identified in the GSIS billing, the amounts withheld from contributions
and loan deductions, and the corresponding remittances as reflected in
Tables No. 24 and 25; and
b. conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions
and loan amortizations by comparing GSIS billings with actual payroll
deductions and remitted amounts. This practice ensures billing
accuracy, facilitates timely remittances to GSIS, and guarantees
correct salary deductions.

12.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounting
records to verify the accuracy of the unaccounted/unsettled "Due to
PhilHealth" account, which has a balance of ₱77,662.80. Additionally, the
unsettled balance per the Statement of Premium Account (SPA) from
PhilHealth, totaling ₱849,627.52, should be examined. Any confirmed
outstanding amounts must be remitted to PhilHealth without delay.

12.6. We also recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer
promptly coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to update employees'
employment status and basic monthly salary. This will ensure that PhilHealth
records reflect accurate and up-to-date information.

12.7. Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. ensure the full and timely remittance of both employee contributions
and the employer’s share in compliance with PhilHealth's deadlines.
Additionally, any interest charges incurred due to delayed payments in
February and April 2024 should be settled immediately using personal
funds; and
b. deduct in the payroll the contribution for PhilHealth premiums of Mr.
Joanis F. Alfafara, and remit the same to PhilHealth, with the employer
share.

13.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a.) Limit the hiring of Job Order workers to indispensable functions only
and pay the prescribed daily minimum wage as prescribed by the
Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Boards for Region VIII;
b.) Prioritize the hiring for vacant Plantilla Positions, subject to the
prescribed PS limitation.

14.11. We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
and the Social Welfare Officers submit the necessary supporting computation
and documentation to support their claim, such as a travel report or service
report, and a copy of the special order from the Local Chief Executive
assigning them to hazardous or difficult areas, or any other applicable
documents. This recommendation is made without prejudice to the issuance of
a notice of suspension or disallowance, should the circumstances warrant.

14.12. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough
review of the hazard pay claim, ensuring it is fully supported by the required
and complete documentation, before any payment is made.

1.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LGSO submit written
justification for the above noted deficiencies.
1.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile her PPE
records, maintain a complete and detailed PPE Ledger cards and provide the
LGSO with the detailed reconciled balance as basis for the physical count.
1.12. We further recommended that the LGSO, upon submission by the Municipal
Accountant of the reconciled balance, reconcile with her property balance as
basis for her physical count.
1.13. During the exit conference, the LGSO and the Municipal Accountant agreed with
the audit recommendations. The LGSO also explained that they have submitted
request for one time cleansing of the missing PPEs which correspond to the
unreconciled differences.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make correcting entry to
revert the amount of ₱1,061,835.00 to the Due to NGAs account. We reiterate
our previous year’s recommendation that income should only be recognized
upon liquidation.
2.10. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant committed to make the
necessary adjustments.

3.12. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant a monthly Schedule of Unreleased Checks for
monitoring of the actual Cash balance in the books.
3.13. We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant prepare a
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to restore the unreleased checks and cancelled
checks to the Cash in bank account based on the schedule submitted by the
Municipal Treasurer, to reflect the actual Cash balance of the LGU at the end
of the year. Said entries shall be reversed when checks are actually released to
the payees in the ensuing year.
3.14. During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

4.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
correcting entries to set the accounts in order. Henceforth, record assets and
payables only if supported with complete documents to prove that goods are
actually delivered or services had been rendered.
4.10. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant committed to make the
necessary accounting entries.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM
Council, and the MDRRM Officer:
a.) Implement during the year the remaining 3 PPAs identified in the
LDRRMFIP in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of
the municipality during natural calamities;
b.) Provide justification for the non-implementation of the remaining
PPAs; and
c.) Provide specific timelines and implement the PPAs under the
LDRRMFIP within the said timelines.
5.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer and record the
unexpended DRRM Fund amounting to ₱2,355,305.15 as Trust Liabilities -
DRRMF under the Special Trust Fund and maintain subsidiary ledgers by
year of transfer.
5.12 During the exit conference, the LDRRMO indicated that she would comply with
the audit recommendations. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant committed to
make the accounting entry.

6.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the appropriate account
in recording expenses and always refer to the Revised Chart of Accounts for
LGUs and their description for proper account title usage.
6.7. During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant committed to comply with
the recommendation.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Officer-in-Charge of the
Municipal Infirmary set limits on expenditures for medicines, food, office,
medical and laboratory supplies of the Municipal Infirmary based on the
trend of their regular annual actual collections from PhilHealth in the prior
years.
7.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from paying prior
year’s obligations/payables using the current year’s appropriation and adhere
to the related provisions in RA 7160.
7.11 During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer or Office-in-Charge explained
that indeed there was no budget in the General Fund allocated for infirmary
supplies. Nevertheless, the LGU agreed with the audit recommendations.

8.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned fast
track the implementation of the remaining two unimplemented development
projects and continuously adhere to the provisions of the aforementioned
DBM-DOF-DILG JMC, to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes of the
Municipality are achieved and the intended purposes of the 20% Development
Fund are realized.
8.10 During the exit conference, the LGU agreed with the audit recommendation.

9.9. We recommended that the Local Development Council revisit the
unimplemented projects and re-appropriate the balances of completed and
unnecessary projects to other PPAs so that funds are optimally utilized to help
achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes of the LGU.
9.10. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer,
identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimum utilization of
the 20% Development Fund.
9.11. During the exit conference, the LGU agreed to comply with the audit
recommendations. Engr. Fernando B. Sarile, Jr., the MPDO, committed to look into
the balances of completed projects which can be used for other priority projects.

10.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned personnel
undertake measures to ensure immediate completion of the project.
10.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, MPDO and Municipal
Engineer submit written justifications on the continuation of the project
despite the lapse of December 2019 as stipulated in the MOA and
commencement of construction even before the release of the Deed of Donation
and acquisition of ECC and Development Permit.
10.16. Moreover, we recommended that the LGU immediately process the titling of
the Land to avoid further delay in the processing of future progress billings.
10.17. Lastly, we recommended that the LGU only undertake projects that are
“implementation ready” in order to provide timely benefits to the intended
beneficiaries.
10.18. During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer explained that they
communicated with the NHA which ordered to continue, fast-track and finish this
project by this year, 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher to record the valid book reconciling item per Bank Reconciliation Statements on the month that they occurred or detected in order that an accurate and reliable cash balance is presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.reason:
The difference between the amount deducted on payroll and on the amount billed by the GSIS to the LGU are the following:

(a) For premiums, the amount deducted on the payroll is the updated salary of the employees. Updating of the GSIS records when it comes to increase or changes in salaries according to our AAO takes time. So the amount billed for premiums and amount deducted is not equal.                              (b) For loan amortizations, the difference is due to the cut-off date that the billing is given to the LGU and the time the employee renews their loan.

2.17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor communicate with the donor,
JVR Foundation, Inc. for the necessary documents relative to the donation of
the motor vehicles.
2.18. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books
the donated Rescue Vehicle, including its depreciation for CY 2021
2.19. Further, we recommended that the General Services Officer transfer the
ownership of the vehicle in the LTO Records from Provincial Government of
Leyte to the Municipal Government of Javier. Furthermore, we recommend
that the Municipal Accountant and GSO do the same for the received BAJAJ
Motor Vehicles once copy of the perfected Deed of Donation is received from
the Donor.
2.20. Moving forward, we recommended that PAR should be issued immediately to
the person in-charge of the vehicle upon his receipt of the said asset.

3.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entries
to reclassify the noted CIP - Infrastructure Assets and CIP - Bldgs. and Other
Structures accounts to their proper PPE accounts, transfer those from Trust
Fund to the General Fund and to recognize the appropriate Depreciation
Expense and corresponding Accumulated Depreciation in the books.
3.9. Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize
the appropriate depreciation expense of the LGU’s assets from the time these
are acquired or completed.

4.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record as Due to NGAs all
receipts from NGAs for specified program or project and only recognize
income upon liquidation.
4.11. The Municipal Accountant agreed with the recommendation and stated that she will
follow the correct entries in recording all future financial assistance that the LGU
will be receiving to avoid misstatements.

5.12. We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer submit proof of actual
utilization of the above inventories already recorded as expense to avoid
suspension in Audit.
5.13. We also recommended that the GSO and personnel in charge of
distribution/issuance of Inventories should:
5.13.1 issue items from Stockroom based on completely filled out Requisition
and Issue Slip;
5.13.2. summarize all RIS in the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of
the following month as basis in recognizing the expenses.
5.14. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
5.14.1. stop recording to expenses the procured supplies and materials
unless it is supported with an SSMI and RIS from the designated
Property Officer/Personnel in charge of distribution/issuance; and
5.14.2. prepare Journal Entries to restore the Disaster Rescue and
Response Equipment amounting P268,150.00 which were recorded
as expense and to recognize the Depreciation Expense – Disaster
Response and Rescue Equipment amounting to P3,927.85.

6.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry
Vouchers to record the valid book reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation
Statements on the month that they occurred or detected in order that an
accurate and reliable cash balance is presented in the financial statements.

7.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare journal entry
voucher to recognize the understatement of the affected accounts. Moving
forward, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant stop the current
practice of recognizing depreciation only upon payment and start computing
depreciation after the projects have been completed.

8.12. We recommended that the Local Development Council revisit the
unimplemented projects and re-appropriate the balances of completed and
unnecessary projects to other PPAs so that funds are utilized to its optimum
to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes of the LGU.
8.13. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer,
identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimum utilization of
the 20% Development Fund.

9.9 We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer submit an explanation on the
use of the Local DRRM Fund for the purchase of these items not related to
disaster risk reduction and management activities. Henceforth, he shall see to
it that purchase of office equipment, office supplies and other procurements
with purposes other than those related to disaster prevention, preparedness and
mitigation should be charged against the general fund of the LGU.

10.15. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team
explanation why these payments were approved despite absence of the
required supporting documents. Henceforth, ensure that the required
supporting documents of all claims are complete before affixing her signature
in Box A of the disbursement voucher to ensure that only valid and proper
claims are approved for payment.
10.16. We also recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Medical
Director of the Municipal Infirmary submit immediately the lacking
supporting documents to establish propriety of the payments made.

11.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justifications why the
fund was utilized for purposes other than what was intended as per letter from
Department of Budget and Management Secretary Wendel Avisado.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (1) issue a directive to each
official of LGU-Javier, who are responsible in the accounting and reporting
of the Local Road Network of the LGU, to accomplish the procedures
outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008; (2) monitor their compliance
thereon; and (3) submit to COA the correct report at year-end.

Specifically, we recommended that:
1.13.1 The Municipal Assessor inspect and assess the cost of the road lot
component of all road networks within the territorial jurisdiction of
LGU-Javier.
1.13.2 The Municipal Engineer:
a) Inspect, appraise and segregate each component of each road
network of the LGU using as initial guide the list from the
accounting records;
b) Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
(RPCLRN) with the complete description and cost of each road
network and its component; and
c) Submit the RPCLRN to COA every year-end, copy furnished the
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer.
1.13.3 The Municipal Accountant:
a) Record the Road Lot component of Municipal Road projects
based on the report of the Municipal Assessor;
b) Reconcile the composition of the Road Network account per books
against the RPCLRN and adjust, where necessary;
c) Maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards with complete
data/description of each component of the; and
d) Disclose in the Notes to FS in the ensuing years the breakdown of
the Road Network account.
1.13.4 The General Services Officer prepare and maintain complete
Property Cards on Road Networks and its components and for every
future procurement of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher and other necessary supporting documents to transfer from the
General Fund to the Special Trust Fund, using the correct accounts, the total
unused CY 2019 QRF and MF-MOOE, and to make the transfer in the
succeeding years prior to the closing of the books.

We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer of the LGU include in the
Annual Investment Plan for CY 2020 the projects, programs and activities to
be funded from the CY 2019 and prior years’ balances.

Vouchers to record all valid book reconciling items as a result of the Bank
Reconciliation Statements, including the reversion of staled checks to present
an accurate cash balance in the books of the LGU.

confirm its status. In the absence of any on-going or pending programs,
return the amounts, including all unutilized balances of completed projects,

of Treasury.

from Trust Fund main.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the Local
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning Development Officer,
exclude in the Annual Investment Plan, projects which are for administrative
purposes.

the 20% Development Fund for projects which are for administrative
purposes and charge it to the General Fund instead.

reconcile the records of each LGU employee with the GSIS; (2) inform
immediately the GSIS of any change in the employees’ salaries and
employment status, and; (3) tally LGU records on premium contributions

payroll preparer immediately, preferably at the start of the month, with
copies of the GSIS billings to ensure that the correct amount of monthly
premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted in the
employee’s salary in the payrolls.

We recommended that, the personnel assigned to prepare the monthly
Payroll, follow the new rates on PHIC premium contribution in computing
the personal and government shares of all regular employees.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the amounts
deducted in the payroll on monthly premium contributions and reconcile the

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) constitute and activate the Local Finance Committee which will update
the rates of taxes and fees provided in the current Tax Ordinance to
maximize the income generation capabilities of the LGU; and

minimum wage of Job Order workers hired by the LGU as prescribed by the

contracts the specific works of each JO which should not duplicate the

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of LGU properties, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer for identification of on-going projects.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Service Officer continue and fast track the reconciliation of the balances of the Accounting Records with property records and verify any discrepancies noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council through the Mayor as Chairman and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, include only in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality projects which are investment or capital in nature so as to maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund and provide the citizens of the municipality with the benefits that the projects can provide.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund with social, economic and environmental development outcomes and those identified in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to implement fully the activities in the LDRRMFIP in order to utilize the 70% mitigation fund in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and equip the LGU officials and its constituents the skill and knowledge to prepare and manage disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and reconcile monthly taxes withheld to ensure that the entire amount is remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within the prescribed due date.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile the LGU records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions.

We also recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) furnish monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommended that the Local School Board implement fully the programs and projects incorporated in its plan and budget in order to maximize the utilization of SEF in accordance with its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) identify programs, projects and activities that will protect children’s rights and welfare with an appropriation equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute and strengthen the created GAD Focal Point System and require said officials to actively perform their duties and functions in order to implement effectively and efficiently the identified GAD programs, projects and activities, ensure alignment with the guidelines on the localization of Magna Carta for Women (MCW) and address the gender issues and concerns in the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint the Municipal Administrator pursuant to the provisions of RA 7160 and the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations in order not to constrict his functions. We also recommended the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer stop paying wages in advance and other allowance not specified in the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor :
1. Submit the list of Punong Barangays who received the housing units and the report on the status of the materials, except those mentioned herein.
2. Submit justification as to why there were constructed units which are being used for private purposes other than housing, such as the Javier Dairy Buffalo Farm.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan, through the Presiding Officer, submit justification on the following:
a.) why no amount was appropriated in the CY 2016 and CY 2017 budget of LGU Javier for the purchase of a resettlement site, despite its commitment when the MOU was signed in September, 2015;
b.) why the Masterlist of Households recipients were not determined and approved when the materials were delivered to the LGU.

We recommend that management stop utilizing 20% Development Fund for the payment of maintenance and other operating expenses. Strictly observe compliance to the provisions of DILG- DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 11, 2011.

We recommend prudence in the utilization and use of government funds be exercised and observed at all times by management to prevent incurrence of unnecessary, irregular, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures.

We recommend that the LCE should instruct the Municipal Accountant in strictly remitting the accurate/exact amount of premiums, contributions and loan payments to the Government Insurance System (GSIS) pursuant to Section 5 (c) of Republic Act 8291 also known as Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997.
We recommend for the refund of the amount Php819,586.15 disbursed from the Priority Development Assistance Fund by the concerned officials/personnel.

a) The appropriate Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for the implementation of YamansaGulay an Project involving an amount of P3,153,638.04 entered into by the Local Government Unit with ASSISI Development Foundation, Inc. (ADFI) was not submitted for audit contrary to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, thus, the transactions were not evaluated as to validity and propriety prior to their payment. b) The procurement of the farm inputs, fuel, hotel room accommodation and other items to implement the Operation YamansaGulay an (OYG) Project in the amount of P880,718.43 were not done through a competitive public bidding contrary to Section 10 Rule IV of the IRR of RA 9184 instead the direct contracting mode was adopted thus costs of the transactions are not assured to be economical.
observations.reason: There was no submitted MOA and justification to the team in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all requests for the procurement of medicines by the Municipal Government should be supported among others with the following: a) A certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being requisitioned or procured conformed with PNDF Volume I, current edition; b) Certificate of Product Registration from the FDA; c) Certification from the manufacturer that the supplier is an authorized distributor/dealer of the medicines. Likewise, the Municipal Health Officer shall have a copy of the latest edition of the PNDF, Volume I as guide in their future procurements of drugs and medicines at the same time prevent the suspension or disallowance of transactions during audits.
observations.reason: Documents were submitted in calendar year 2017.

observations.recommendation:
(1) create an Inventory Committee and require them to conduct the annual
physical inventory of all PPEs of the LGU;
(2) require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property
Cards (PCs) of all PPEs to include data on the quantity, condition, date of
acquisition and acquisition cost, and;
(3) require the Municipal Accountant to update the Property Ledger Cards and
reconcile the same with the records of the General Services Office.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and
project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects
funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided
for in the MOA and require the immediate submission of Liquidation Reports
and other documents to the Source Agencies.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor utilize the 20% Development
Fund only for the implementation of environmental, socio-economic
development projects. Also, mandate the Local Development Council to include
in the AIP only capital expenditures for the development of the municipality.

We recommend that the Mayor require the requesting barangays to sign
a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the municipality supported with a
Budget Proposals/Program of Work and approved SB Resolutions before funds
are transferred to the recipient barangays. The MOA should stipulate that the
recipient barangays should submit liquidation documents immediately after the
purpose has been accomplished.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all recipient
barangays to submit Fund Utilization Report (FUR) together with the necessary
attachments, as soon as the intended program/project is implemented and defer
the grant of additional financial assistance unless the previous financial
assistance have been liquidated.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board of Javier, Leyte,
through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, maximize the utilization of the SEF
for the improvement of educational facilities and other priority
programs/projects/activities enumerated in DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular
No. 1 s. 1998 to ensure that the desired benefits are availed by the students.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
a. secure a copy of the Purchaser’s Permit from the National
Telecommunication Commission (NTC) to consider the purchase of
Portable Radio Transceivers and Accessories a valid and legitimate
expenditure and prevent the issuance of a Notice of Suspension.
b. require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit to the Audit
Team documents relative to the bidding of the Portable Radio
Transceivers and Accessories.
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to process claims for payment only
when the Disbursement Voucher is completely supported with the
necessary documents.

recommend strict compliance with provisions of the law and rules governing cash advances. We reiterate compliance with the hereunder course of actions, to wit: a. A demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2013 from concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; b. Strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; and c. Immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on the settlement and liquidations of all accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: As of CY 2017, the amount is decreased already since the management resulted to withholding of Terminal Leave Credits of concerned accountable officers and employees.
observations.reason: amounting to P322,701.32 remained unliquidated as at year, thus, funds are exposed to loss or misuse while the concerned personnel who could face sanction as consequence in addition to the suspension or disallowance of their respective delinq uent accounts, said circumstances likewise affecting misstatements of affected expenses as well as asset accounts per the agency’s financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV and make necessary adjustment pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on submission of perfected contracts and purchase orders and supporting documents to COA for the conduct of auditorial and legal reviews on pertinent transactions. This practice shall be regularly complied with for transactions to be assured of being valid and proper prior to payments.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the rules on submission on time of paid documents. This has to be done not only to protect claims from being suspense or disallowed due to doubtful validity as well as propriety but to avoid the legal consequences of non-submission of accounts. Moreover, management shall further direct the immediate submission of the remaining twenty-five (25) paid Disbursement Vouchers together with their corresponding supporting documents to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance during post-audit.

Management should require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Accountant Designate to strictly comply with the documentary requirements stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in relation to the submission of contracts and disbursement supporting documents.

We recommend the submission of the (i) Program of Work duly approved by the DILG Regional Office; (ii) “No Build Zone” certification;(iii) Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a MOA.

We recommend that management, through its Focal Point Chairperson prepares and submits its GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC should: a) Compel the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all the PPMPs into an APP within ten (10) days after the approval of the agency budget. Said APP shall be submitted to the BAC for certification and thereafter to the Municipal Mayor for approval; b) Conduct a public bidding on all needed goods of the agency in a quarterly basis in accordance with approved APP; c) If bidding is not practical the agency may proceed to the adoption of other modes of procurement but only if recourse to PS-DBM is made and stocks needed are not available thereat particularly in the case of common-used supplies. The certification that stocks are not available in said office will validate procurements from other bonafide procurements suppliers.
We recommend that management ensures the existence of a valid Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement and resolution form the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the transfer or receipt of funds.

We recommend that management to instruct the concerned officers and employees of the agency to comply with the deficiencies noted on the transactions to avoid being suspended and disallowed in audit and; prepare and submit Monthly Summary of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle owned by the LGU supported with corresponding RIS for gasoline and duly accomplished drivers trip ticket.

We recommend that management, through its GAD Focal Point Chairperson, thoroughly prepares its GAD Plan and Budget and submit the same for review and approval by the DILG Regional Office in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to ensure that all GAD related expenditures are aligned to the GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer returns the unexpended balance amounting to PDAF to the National Treasury.
observations.reason: There was already a check for the return but it is still not yet deposited to National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant for the absolute stoppage of procurement thru reimbursements since it is not a valid mode of acquiring supplies and materials.

We recommend that the LCE should instruct the Municipal Accountant in strictly deduct the withholding taxes and remit immediately the taxes withheld from the supplier of an internet connection to the LGU concerned and henceforth observe the BIR regulation to remit on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the Municipality to faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the submission of paid vouchers, payrolls, collection receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 19, 1995.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of account provided for in Section 107 and Section 122 of Presidential Decree 1445 for well-timed audit and review of LGUs transactions and prompt/quick delivery of audit results to concerned LGU officials and officers.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should create a committee to conduct the physical inventory of the municipality’s property, plant and equipment at least once a year and a committee to prepare an inventory report for reconciliation purposes. Likewise, the municipal treasurer should maintain property cards per category of property, plant and equipment in order to determine the unserviceable and non-existent properties for eventual dropping of these in the PPE accounts pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386, Section 119 and 124 of NGAs Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has documented actual inventory per department but had not yet assigned a committee to conduct the physical inventory of the LGU’s PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Chief Executive coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer for the preparation and implementation of R.A. 9003 also known as “Solid Waste Management Act of 2000”.

We recommend that management, through its GAD Focal Point Chairperson, thoroughly prepares its GAD Plan and Budget and submit the same for review and approval by the DILG Regional Office in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

Considering that there is a prescribe period of five (5) years, and to protect said amount against misuse, we recommend that concerned employee be instructed to: a. The Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with the mandate on transfer of unexpended fund to a special trust fund. b. The Municipal Accountant should record said transfer in the books of accounts. c. Actual fund transfers shall likewise be carried out from the General to the Trust Fund, said funds to be used for the intended purpose for which they were created in pursuance with the law on DRRMF and its implementing rules. d. Revert to the General Fund unutilized MDRRMF after five (5) years and be made available for other social services to be identified by the local sanggunian.

We recommend for strict compliance in the submission of Utilization Reports to the pertinent offices and the COA, in effect, capacitate the agency to present outright information regarding the MDRRMF and make possible the assessment thru audit of the propriety as well as the validity of disbursements made out of the funds.
observations.managementAction: There was partial submission. The LGU was submitting Utilization Reports of DRRM to DILG but failed to submit the said utilization report to COA every quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statement of the agency.
We recommend that the Accountant prepares a JEV to revert the unexpended balance of the PDAF amounting to P498,978.59 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality to faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the submission of paid vouchers, payrolls, collection receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend for the submission of the (i) Program of Work duly approved by the DILG Regional Office; (ii) “No Build Zone, (iii) Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a MOA; and (iv) duly signed Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and DILG Regional Office.

We recommend that management allocate funds for the insurance of the agency’s building with the GSIS to ensure indemnification once properties are lost or damaged either thru man-made or natural causes, said course of action also to be carried out since mandated under relevant laws and rules. It is further recommended that management provide their properties with adequate protection from risks of calamities like fire, typhoon and earthquake by securing comprehensive insurance.

We recommend for management to exhaustively initiate all remedies for the cash advances to be liquidated and settled. This shall be duly complied with to avert the legal consequences of committing the deficiency. Furthermore in the grant, use and liquidation of cash advance funds, strict adherence to relevant provision of COA circular No. 97-002 as well as other pertinent rules shall be strictly observed, thus: a. For those cash advances which remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2013, the Municipal Accountant shall demand for the liquidation from the concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; b. Update regularly the Subsidiary Ledgers for all cash advances; and c. The Municipal Accountant should have a complete file of Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents for all unliquidated cash advances, by labelling it as “Active Cash Advances”, which will support the Subsidiary Ledgers and the Aging of Cash Advances.

a. The Chairman of the BAC should: 1) Compel the BAC secretariat to consolidate all the PPMPs into an APP within ten (10) days after the approval of the agency budget. Said APP shall be submitted to the BAC for certification and thereafter to the Municipal Mayor for approval; 2) Conduct a public bidding on all needed goods of the agency in a quarterly basis in accordance with approved APP; 3) If bidding is not practical the agency may proceed to the adoption of other modes of procurement but only if recourse to PS-DBM is made and stocks needed are not available thereat particularly in the case of common-used supplies. The certification that stocks are not available in said office will validate procurements from other bonafide suppliers. b. We recommend for the absolute stoppage of procurements thru reimbursements since not a valid mode of acquiring supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: There was already a commencement of correction of the agency’s entire procurement process. This measure is one of those that are still yet to be fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Chairman of the BAC should: a) Prepare the JEV based on the SSMI to be maintained by the Municipal Treasurer or the General Services Officer; b) Maintain an updated Supplies Ledger Card and reconcile periodically the balances appearing therein with the supplies stock cards maintained by the Municipal Treasurer or the General Services Officer as the case maybe.

We recommend for strict compliance with the hereunder courses of action, thus: Contracts, POs and MOAs should be regularly submitted to COA for auditorial review as to legality and propriety including technical evaluation for reasonableness of cost and contract duration. The personnel directly responsible for the required submission shall be instructed to regularly carry out the required procedure as part of management practice. This has to be duly implemented to avoid the legal consequences of not complying with the mandates of the pertinent COA rule.
We recommend that claims will only be paid upon presentation of complete documentation as required by regulations to avoid suspensions of transactions in audit.

To achieve on-time submission of accounts, we recommend that the Treasury and Accountant be directed to promptly turn-over documents, reports and journals under their possession for the prescribed time which shall be duly covered by transmittal letters, in turn, cause the prompt submission for audit. In addition, said transmittal letters shall prove the compliance of each to their duties over the agency’s accounts.

We recommend that management: a) Record regular purchases of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method; b) Conduct regular count of supplies and materials on hand; c) Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, supplies ledger cards and stock accounts under the agency’s books; d) Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms and ensure coordination between the end-users and the accounting office for the submission of required reports and the proper accounting for the supplies inventory.
observations.managementAction: Management commenced the correction of the agency’s entire procurement process. This measure is one of those that are still yet to be fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accounting office be strictly directed to record expenses using the correct account title and code, otherwise, if continued, the use of the “Other MOE” may erroneously be considered intentional and not in conformity with the mandated honest as well as objective recording of transactions.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer secures from claimants complete documents prior to release of funds.
